from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    # Helper to format numeric objects with commas and strip trailing .0
    def _fmt(val: str) -> str:
        try:
            num = float(val)
            if num.is_integer():
                return f"{int(num):,}"
            else:
                # keep one decimal if needed
                return f"{num:,}"
        except ValueError:
            return val.strip('"')

    templates = {
        # Airport‑specific predicates
        "cityServed": "serves {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": "is {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is {obj} feet above sea level",
        # more natural phrasing with explicit units
        "runwayLength": "runway length is {obj} metres",
        "runwayName": "runway name is {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "ICAO code is {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "location identifier is {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "IATA code is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of {obj} metres",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "first runway surface is {obj}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "first runway number is {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "runway surface is {obj}",
        # Generic entity predicates
        "leader": "leader is {obj}",
        "capital": "capital is {obj}",
        "language": "language is {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {obj}",
        "currency": "currency is {obj}",
        "owner": "owner is {obj}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {obj}",
        "founder": "was founded by {obj}",
        "postalCode": "postal code is {obj}",
        "areaCode": "area code is {obj}",
        "largestCity": "largest city is {obj}",
        "countySeat": "county seat is {obj}",
        "mayor": "mayor is {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "leader party is {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title is {obj}",
        "chief": "chief is {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "hub airport is {obj}",
        "regionServed": "serves region {obj}",
        "nativeName": "native name is {obj}",
        "jurisdiction": "has jurisdiction in {obj}",
        # Biological classification predicates
        "class": "class is {obj}",
        "division": "division is {obj}",
        "order": "order is {obj}",
        "species": "species is {obj}",
        "demonym": "demonym is {obj}",
    }

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        # collect objects per predicate
        pred_objs = {}
        for t in tlist:
            pred_objs.setdefault(t.predicate, []).append(t.object.strip('"'))

        phrase_parts = []
        for pred, objs in pred_objs.items():
            # special handling for capital
            if pred == "capital":
                phrase_parts.append(f"The capital of {subject} is {objs[0]}")
                continue
            # special handling for leader (multiple possible)
            if pred == "leader":
                if len(objs) > 1:
                    joined = " and ".join(objs)
                    phrase_parts.append(f"{joined} are leaders of {subject}")
                else:
                    phrase_parts.append(f"The leader of {subject} is {objs[0]}")
                continue
            # generic handling using templates
            tmpl = templates.get(pred)
            if tmpl:
                # if template expects subject, fill later; otherwise just object
                if "{subject}" in tmpl:
                    phrase_parts.append(tmpl.format(subject=subject, obj=objs[0]))
                else:
                    phrase_parts.append(tmpl.format(obj=objs[0]))
            else:
                # fallback generic phrase
                pred_readable = pred.replace('_', ' ')
                phrase_parts.append(f"{pred_readable} {objs[0]}")

        if not phrase_parts:
            continue

        # Build a fluent sentence for the subject
        if len(phrase_parts) == 1:
            sentence = f"{subject} {phrase_parts[0]}."
        else:
            sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(phrase_parts[:-1]) + ", and " + phrase_parts[-1] + "."
        sentences.append(sentence)

    return " ".join(sentences)

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
